# shewhart

> Statistical process control (SPC) for Python: control charts (I-MR, Xbar-R,
> Xbar-S, p, np, c, u, Laney p'/u', EWMA, CUSUM, run chart) with
> Nelson/Western Electric rules, process capability (Cp/Cpk/Pp/Ppk) with
> confidence intervals and non-normal methods, tolerance intervals, and a
> one-call review() that selects the chart and returns a structured verdict.
> Validated against NIST reference data in CI. pandas in, Result out; built
> for headless/cron/agent use. MIT license. Install: pip install shewhart

Canonical usage (always `import shewhart as sw`):

    import shewhart as sw
    rv = sw.review(df, value="torque", lsl=9.95, usl=10.05)  # one call:
    rv.ok          # chart selection + assumption checks + capability gate
    rv.to_dict()   # structured verdict (JSON-safe), for pipelines and agents

    r = sw.imr(df, value="torque", rules="nelson")     # individuals chart
    r = sw.xbar_r(df, value="x", subgroup="batch")     # subgrouped chart
    r = sw.p_chart(df, defectives="rejects", size="inspected")
    r = sw.laney_p(df, defectives="rejects", size="inspected")  # overdispersion
    r = sw.ewma(df, value="x", lam=0.2)
    r = sw.cusum(df, value="x", k=0.5, h=4)            # tabular CUSUM
    r = sw.capability(df, value="dia", lsl=9.95, usl=10.05)  # has cpk_lci/cpk_uci
    r = sw.capability(df, value="dia", usl=2.0, dist="auto") # non-normal
    r = sw.tolerance_interval(df, value="x", coverage=0.99)
    r.ok          # False if any rule fired (use as exit code)
    r.summary()   # plain-text verdict
    r.table       # per-point DataFrame with signal flags
    r.plot(); r.to_html("report.html")
    sw.report([r1, r2], "weekly.html")                 # combined HTML report

Phase I/II workflow (fit once, monitor forever):

    import sys
    sw.review(df_hist, value="x").baseline.save("baseline.json")
    rv = sw.review(df_new, value="x", limits="baseline.json")
    sys.exit(0 if rv.ok else 1)

Key facts: within-sigma uses moving range / d2 (NOT x.std(), which is the
overall sigma and defines Pp/Ppk); rule sets are "nelson",
"western_electric", "none"; attribute charts apply only the four attribute
tests; EWMA/CUSUM apply limit checks only (run rules assume independence);
capability includes a stability gate and Anderson-Darling note; review()
never invents statistics - it composes the validated functions and the
selection conventions are documented.

## Docs

- [API and grammar](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/reference/api/): complete frozen surface
- [One-call SPC review](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/review/): decision tree, checks, verdict schema
- [Statistics is not a language task](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/agents/): why agents should call tools, not generate formulas
- [Control charts in Python](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/control-charts-in-python/)
- [Cpk with confidence intervals](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/cpk-in-python/)
- [Nelson rules](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/nelson-rules/)
- [Laney p' charts](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/laney-p-chart/)
- [SPC report as cron job](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/guides/spc-report-cron/)
- [Validation](https://bertanucar.github.io/shewhart/reference/validation/): NIST anchors, policy

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/bertanucar/shewhart)
- Install: pip install shewhart
